WORKORDER.table_comment: IBM Maximo work-order header. One row per WO holding CURRENT state. Status history is in WOSTATUS. Filter WOCLASS = 'WORKORDER' for normal WOs (table also holds PM, CHANGE, RELEASE, ACTIVITY records).

columns.WONUM: Business key — unique within SITEID, not globally.
columns.SITEID: Site code. Always include in joins between Maximo tables — WONUM is unique only within a site.
columns.STATUS: Current status. For history (transitions, time-in-state), use WOSTATUS.
columns.STATUSDATE: When the current STATUS was set (not original create — use REPORTDATE for create time).
columns.WOCLASS: Filter to 'WORKORDER' for normal WOs. Other values: PM, CHANGE, RELEASE, ACTIVITY.
columns.ISTASK: 1 = child task. Deduplicate to parent (PARENT column) for backlog counts.
columns.PARENT: Parent WONUM if this is a child task (ISTASK=1) or sub-WO.
columns.PMNUM: FK to PM when this WO was generated by a PM.
columns.REPORTDATE: When the WO was created/reported. Use this for backlog age, not STATUSDATE.
columns.FAILURECODE: Top-level failure class code (FK to FAILURECODE table).

WOSTATUS.table_comment: Work-order status history — one row per status transition per WO. Required for 'time in status' / 'transition path' / 'who changed when' questions. Note: REST-API status changes may NOT write to this table on classic Maximo 7.6 (see APAR IJ17261).

columns.MEMO: Optional free-text memo entered with the change.

ASSET.table_comment: Asset master. Join via ASSETNUM + SITEID (composite key).

columns.CLASSSTRUCTUREID: FK to CLASSSTRUCTURE — the asset class taxonomy.
columns.LOCATION: Where the asset currently resides (FK to LOCATIONS).

ASSETMETER.table_comment: Meter definitions per asset — corrosion thickness gauges, vibration channels, runtime hours, etc. Readings live in METERREADING. Meter type is a property of the METER definition, not stored here. Condition-monitoring warn/action limits live on MEASUREPOINT.

MEASUREPOINT.table_comment: Condition-monitoring measurement points — per-asset meter points with warning/action limits that trigger PM or WO generation when breached. Readings flow in via METERREADING against the associated meter.

columns.METERNAME: FK to ASSETMETER — the meter this measurement point monitors.

METERREADING.table_comment: Time-series meter readings against ASSETMETER. Append-only; ordered by READINGDATE.

LOCATIONS.table_comment: Location master. Hierarchy traversal via LOCHIERARCHY table for multi-level structures.

columns.TYPE: Location type (OPERATING, STOREROOM, LABOR, COURIER, REPAIR, SALVAGE, VENDOR).

LABTRANS.table_comment: Labor transactions — one row per craft-hour booked to a WO. Aggregate to WO grain via WONUM + SITEID for actuals.

PM.table_comment: Preventive maintenance master — schedule and rules for generating WOs. PMSEQUENCE holds the step sequences.

columns.JPNUM: FK to JOBPLAN — the template used to generate WOs.

FAILURECODE.table_comment: Failure code taxonomy — a tree of PROBLEM, CAUSE, and REMEDY nodes. Aggregate by flattening to the level the user asked for.

FAILUREREPORT.table_comment: Per-WO coded failure record. Created when a WO is closed with failure information.

LABOR.table_comment: IBM Maximo labor master — one row per maintainable resource (employee or contractor). Joined by LABORCODE from LABTRANS, ASSIGNMENT, WORKORDER.LEAD. See the maximo-labor-resources skill for the full domain.

columns.PERSONID: FK to PERSON. NULL for contractor resources that have no PERSON link.
columns.STATUS: ACTIVE or INACTIVE. Only ACTIVE can be assigned new work.
columns.VENDOR: FK to COMPANIES — populated for contractor labor (the contractor flag; there is no LABOR.OUTSIDELABOR column).

PERSON.table_comment: Person master. PII-sensitive — handle names/emails/phone with care. See the maximo-labor-resources skill for usage patterns.

columns.SUPERVISOR: FK to PERSON.PERSONID — chain of supervision.

LABORCRAFTRATE.table_comment: Pay rates per (labor, craft, skill level). Currency varies — do not aggregate RATE across CURRENCYCODE without normalizing.

columns.CURRENCYCODE: Currency — be careful aggregating across currencies.

QUALIFICATION.table_comment: Qualification catalog. PERSONS hold qualifications via QUALPERSON. See the maximo-labor-resources skill.

columns.CRAFT: Optional — qualification tied to a craft.
columns.REQUIREDFORWORK: 1 if the qualification is mandatory for matched work.

QUALPERSON.table_comment: Person ↔ Qualification with expiry. For 'qualified labor' queries, filter STATUS='ACTIVE' AND (EXPIRYDATE IS NULL OR EXPIRYDATE > current_date()).

columns.EXPIRYDATE: When it lapses (NULL = never). Filter to keep current certs only.

CERTIFICATION.table_comment: Certification catalog (in some customer models, a subset of QUALIFICATION).

AMCREW.table_comment: Crew master. Crews are time-bound groupings of labor — see AMCREWLABOR for membership. Keyed on (ORGID, AMCREW).

columns.AMCREW: Crew identifier (business key, unique within ORGID).
columns.CREWTYPE: FK to the crew-type master AMCREWT (required craft mix).

AMCREWLABOR.table_comment: Crew composition. Time-bounded — EFFECTIVEDATE/ENDDATE define membership window. For current crew, filter EFFECTIVEDATE <= current_date() AND (ENDDATE IS NULL OR ENDDATE > current_date()). Keyed on AMCREW.

columns.LABORCODE: FK to LABOR — one row per labor member.

PERSONGROUP.table_comment: Named person group. Members in PERSONGROUPTEAM. Used by workflow assignment, paging, on-call rotation. Can nest — beware loops.

CALENDAR.table_comment: Working calendar identifier. Specific work periods (shifts, days off) in WORKPERIOD.

WORKPERIOD.table_comment: Specific work periods per calendar — one row per shift per work date. Often sparsely populated — probe coverage (MIN/MAX shiftstart) before claiming 'we have capacity for X'.

columns.WORKDATE: The calendar date this work period falls on.

ASSIGNMENT.table_comment: Labor ↔ WO assignment. One row per labor scheduled for specific WO work.

columns.LABORCODE: FK to LABOR (NULL if assigned to a CRAFT only, not a specific person).

LOCHIERARCHY.table_comment: Multi-system parent-child for LOCATIONS. One row per (location, parent, systemid). A single location can appear in multiple hierarchies (Operating, Storeroom, Network). Always filter SYSTEMID. See the maximo-asset-hierarchy skill.

LOCANCESTOR.table_comment: Location closure table — one row per (descendant, ancestor) pair at any depth. The IBM-canonical traversal mechanism for hierarchical rollups. Use instead of naïve PARENT self-joins for multi-level analytics.

ASSETANCESTOR.table_comment: Asset closure table — one row per (descendant, ancestor) pair at any depth in the ASSET hierarchy.

columns.ANCESTOR: An ancestor asset at any depth.
columns.HIERARCHYLEVELS: Number of levels between descendant and ancestor (0 = self if convention includes it).

SYSTEM.table_comment: Hierarchy system definition. Each LOCHIERARCHY row belongs to one system. Default is PRIMARY (the operational hierarchy).

columns.PRIMARYSYSTEM: 1 if this is the default system.

CLASSSTRUCTURE.table_comment: Asset / location classification tree. A taxonomy hierarchy applied via CLASSSTRUCTUREID. Separate from LOCATIONS physical hierarchy — these answer different questions. Maximo does not ship a CLASSANCESTOR closure table — use recursive CTE on PARENT.

columns.CLASSIFICATIONID: Full classification path string in some installs.
columns.USEWITH: Whether this class applies to ASSET, LOCATIONS, ITEM, etc.

ASSETSPEC.table_comment: Per-asset specification values, driven by CLASSSPEC at the class level. For analytics like 'show me all pumps with flow rate > 5000 gpm', join ASSETSPEC on ASSETATTRID.
